progress_summary: Invoked in assessment mode (not setup) alongside oc-app-architect to validate a six-part user RCA on opchain's coordination layer. Findings relevant to monitoring-ops: opchain's own observability of its pipeline is non-functional. oc-telemetry-ops advertises telemetry_handle.enabled=true since 2026-06-26 with sink .checkpoints/usage.sqlite, but nothing has ever called telemetry.mjs record (only caller is the package.json alias) and the sqlite store has never been created — 24 days of enabled-with-no-store, undetected. No oc-monitoring-ops checkpoint had ever existed in this repo before this write; oc-security-auditor has never run in repo history. checkpoint.mjs doctor detects 23 drift warnings including a 27-day-stale orchestrator, but doctor is not run by CI and nothing alarms on its output. Deploy/release audit gates are markdown tables with no executable enforcement.
next_actions: Add `checkpoint doctor --fail-on-warnings` (or a drift-budget threshold) to ci.yml — doctor already detects the 27-day orchestrator drift, but nothing runs it, so drift is discoverable and never discovered.

Fix the oc-telemetry-ops liveness lie: either wire an actual `telemetry.mjs record` call site, or make `/oc-telemetry status` fail loudly when enabled=true and the sink is absent. Enabled-with-no-store must not read as healthy.
Treat 'monitoring the pipeline' as a first-class surface: the same enabled-but-silent failure that hit telemetry is the T0 gap in opchain's own observability of opchain.
Re-run /oc-monitor audit after the coordination-gaps patch lands to score the ecosystem's self-observability (currently ungraded — this skill had never run here).

key_decisions: This invocation was assessment-only; no instrumentation, alerts, SLOs, or runbooks were created for opchain.dev.
The user's proposed 'post-merge auto-stamp workflow' fix was found to be a previously-shipped-and-removed anti-pattern (commit 66107d1, 2026-06-22).

SUPERSEDED by adversarial review: client-side hooks are NOT the answer either. `git -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null commit` bypasses a blocking .githooks/pre-commit while a sibling probe still reads ARMED (verified, git 2.50.1). All 6 adversarial verifiers converged instead on a required read-only CI status check on PR head — the only surface that is not per-clone, per-worktree, per-agent, or flag-defeatable, and the only one that reaches the second (Codex) agent.

Binding strength is a property of the SURFACE, not the wording. A tool description is re-presented every model call; a callee SKILL.md is read once and decays. This is why oc-git-ops:223's imperative mandate never fires.
